mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from a restrictive level, the market reads a declining discount-rate path and less policy drag on earnings, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above unconditional baseline after FEDFUNDS has declined for 3 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL monthly inflation breakout forces real-rate repricing and raises the probability of further tightening, so equity multiples compress and forward S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of trigger events across a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent trend signals slowing household income growth and weaker demand, which leads S&P 500 returns lower over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in any sustained 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad consumer demand and housing turnover, which supports revenues and cyclicals, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upswings in retail sales and housing starts fail to outperform baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the signal better than the interaction in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to a stable labor market indicates improving capacity utilization and stronger real activity, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average rises while UNRATE is unchanged or falls
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising industrial production with stable or lower unemployment do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A falling GDPC1 growth rate signals demand deterioration that eventually lowers expected sales and earnings, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if sub-1.0% GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns at or above baseline frequency, or if the signal has no out-of-sample edge over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, firms are losing labor momentum and households face weaker income growth, which should reduce equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative and UNRATE rises at least 0.2 pp over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-loss and unemployment-rise condition does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction adds no explanatory power beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive-rate regime, a flat or rising CPIAUCSL reading keeps real rates elevated and delays policy relief, so S&P 500 returns remain negative over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if, with FEDFUNDS above 5.0%, flat or rising CPI does not coincide with below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect reverses during the same regime in a rolling 2-year test.
